# Tenex Business Performance

Tenex Business Performance Limited is a UK management consultancy helping SME founders and regulated businesses improve performance, adopt AI responsibly, and build businesses worth selling.

## Who we are

Founded by David McWhir, with over 38 years of experience across UK regulated industries including financial services, hospitality, care, manufacturing, professional services and construction. Registered in England and Wales (Company No. 16796831). Based in Leicestershire, UK. Working nationally across the UK.

## What we do

We work with two types of client:
1. Founder-led SMEs that have outgrown the early stages and need to run better
2. Established financial services businesses navigating strategic change — AI adoption, operational transformation, regulatory pressure, or building toward a transaction

Our approach: understand the business first, then act. We identify what is holding performance back before recommending any solution. When AI features in our work, the capability we build stays inside the client's business, not ours.

## The AI adoption problem we solve

Research from Microsoft's Work Trend Index shows only 4% of organisations have both the individual AI capability and the organisational readiness to use it effectively. Most organisations fail in one of three ways:

1. Buy and hope — tools are purchased, nothing changes, because the work is never redesigned
2. Delegate and disconnect — the AI mandate goes to one person or team without the authority to change how the rest of the organisation works
3. Outsource and exit — a consultancy delivers a strategy; the knowledge stays with them, not the client

Tenex is the embedded alternative to all three. We build capability that stays inside the business.

## Services

- Business Performance Review — structured diagnosis of where a business is losing time, money or momentum, with a clear action plan
- AI Strategic Assessment — structured diagnostic to establish AI capability and organisational readiness; identifies which failure modes apply and what to address first
- AI Adoption & Governance — building the organisational infrastructure that makes AI work in a specific business context; not a technology project, a business improvement programme
- AI Chief of Staff — an always-on AI that handles correspondence, diary and daily coordination; built around the client's business, governed from day one, trusted in stages (see /ai-chief-of-staff.html)
- Cyber SAIF — AI security assurance; pen testing, audits and governance reviews for businesses whose AI systems act on their behalf (see /cyber-saif.html)
- Governance, Risk & Compliance — GRC frameworks for regulated sectors that protect the business without creating administrative overhead
- Exit Readiness — building a business that can operate and sell without depending on the founder
- Fractional CRO — Chief Risk Officer capability without the full-time cost; suited to financial services businesses navigating regulatory change or M&A
- Data & MI Architecture — helping businesses own and understand their data, independent of SaaS platforms

## Who we work with

- UK SME founders (typically 10–200 employees) at performance, growth or exit inflection points
- Financial services businesses (FCA/PRA regulated) navigating strategic or regulatory change
- Businesses across financial services, hospitality, care, manufacturing, professional services and construction

## Location

Leicestershire, UK. Serving clients nationally across the United Kingdom.

## Case studies (anonymised)

Three live engagements described in detail at /case-studies.html:

1. Hospitality — multi-site restaurant group: automated MI pipeline, daily flash reporting across 7 sites before 8am, 5 company-wide KPIs, weekly trading and period P&L in development.

2. Professional services / construction — specialist bid consultancy: AI-enabled bid operating system built on Microsoft 365 Copilot, 22-step APMP-validated process, 20% time saving confirmed on live government framework bid, AI governance framework compliant with PPN 017/25.

3. Food manufacturing — growth-stage manufacturer: 124-item employer compliance platform, 3D digital facility model, supply agreement for major export order, financial analysis and governance infrastructure toward a structured exit.
